sourceMovie.title: Eyes Wide Shut
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Eyes Wide Shut (1999) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Donnie Darko
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Eyes Wide Shut clicked for you, Donnie Darko (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and mystery, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Jackie Brown
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Eyes Wide Shut clicked for you, Jackie Brown (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Match Point
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Eyes Wide Shut clicked for you, Match Point (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Eyes Wide Shut?
faq.answer: Try Donnie Darko, Jackie Brown, The Girl on the Train—each shares DNA with Eyes Wide Shut while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Eyes Wide Shut?
faq.answer: Eyes Wide Shut (1999) is best described as Drama, Thriller, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Eyes Wide Shut worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
